title: phlox douglasii subsp. eudouglasii
intro: Phlox douglasii subsp. eudouglasii, also known as Douglas' phlox or wild phlox, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Phlox douglasii subsp. eudouglasii include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
